name: pytest_runner
description: Run Python pytest tests with filtering by name (--grep), verbose output, timeout, output control (--head/--tail), and summary-only mode (--summary-only). Automatically cds to the project root before running pytest.
when_to_use: Use when you need to run Python tests with pytest. Replaces 'cd <project> && python -m pytest ...' sequences. The --grep flag lets you run only relevant tests (e.g., --grep=ddl runs only DDL-related tests). Use --tail to show only the summary at the end, or --head to see the beginning of test output. Use -x to stop on first failure for faster debugging. Use --summary-only to show only passed/failed/error summary lines (replaces the common pattern 'pytest ... | grep -E "passed|failed|ERROR"').

parameters.name: project_root
parameters.description: Root directory of the Python project (e.g., /app)

parameters.name: test_path
parameters.description: Test file, class, or directory path relative to project root (e.g., 'tests/unit_tests/test_query.py::test_func'). Supports multiple paths separated by spaces.

parameters.name: --grep
parameters.description: Filter tests by name pattern (passed as -k to pytest)

parameters.name: --verbose|-v
parameters.description: Verbose output showing individual test names

parameters.name: --head
parameters.description: Show only first N lines of output

parameters.name: --tail
parameters.description: Show only last N lines of output

parameters.name: --no-header
parameters.description: Suppress pytest header for leaner output

parameters.name: --summary-only
parameters.description: Show only summary lines (passed/failed/error/slowest durations). Replaces the common pattern 'pytest ... | grep -E "passed|failed|ERROR"'.

examples.expected: Runs all unit tests with verbose output.
Runs only DDL-related tests in test_query.py with verbose output.
Shows only the last 20 lines of test output (pass/fail summary).
Runs two test files with stop-on-first-failure and quiet mode, showing only last 10 lines.
Runs runnable tests quietly and shows only summary lines (passed/failed/error). Replaces 'pytest -q --no-header ... | grep -E "passed|failed|ERROR"'.

cost_saving_rationale: Replaces 2-3 separate steps (cd to project, run pytest with filtering, pipe through head/tail/grep) with a single script call. The --grep flag eliminates running the full test suite when only specific tests are needed. Built-in --head/--tail flags prevent token overflow from verbose test output. The --summary-only flag replaces the common 'pytest ... | grep -E "passed|failed|ERROR"' pattern (saves 1 tool call for piping through grep) and directly addresses the common need for a quick pass/fail summary. Reduces round trips by batching the cd, test run, and output filtering into one step. The -x and -q flags avoid the need to manually add these common pytest flags.
